title: Fix 6 structural bugs in 8-file document processing workflow
goal:
A document processing workflow has 6 structural bugs across 6 of 8 pipeline files. Each bug requires a structural fix (reorganizing code), not just changing a value.

Bug 1 (classifier.py): `_stopwords` is initialized inside classify() instead of __init__(). This means _compute_score() and _normalize_text() crash with AttributeError when called directly. Fix: move the _stopwords initialization to __init__().

Bug 2 (extractor.py): Cross-field dependency validation happens inside the extraction loop. Fields like 'party' get invalidated because their dependency ('case_number') hasn't been extracted yet in the current iteration. Fix: split into two passes — extract all fields first, then validate deps.

Bug 3 (normalizer.py): Four lines (fields assignment, stage update, counter increment, results.append) are indented one level too deep — inside the inner field loop instead of the outer record loop. This causes duplicate records and inflated counters. Fix: dedent those 4 lines.

Bug 4 (validator.py): _validate_format runs before _validate_required. Format validation skips None fields (returns valid), but bad-format fields get format errors even when required fields are missing. Fix: run _validate_required first, skip _validate_format if any required fields are missing.

Bug 5 (indexer.py): `entries = []` is a class variable (shared between all instances). Fix: move to __init__ as `self.entries = []`.

Bug 6 (reporter.py): Sections are built in dependency order (timeline first) but section_order is derived from dict keys, giving wrong presentation order. Fix: build sections in canonical order: overview, categories, validation, index_stats, field_coverage, timeline.

All 31 tests in test_workflow.py must pass after fixes.
hints: Six structural bugs in six different files. Bug 1: init order. Bug 2: loop splitting. Bug 3: indentation level. Bug 4: execution order. Bug 5: class vs instance variable. Bug 6: dict insertion order. Tests verify each bug independently plus full pipeline integration.

seed_files.workflow/__init__.py:
"""Document processing workflow pipeline."""

from workflow.ingester import Ingester
from workflow.classifier import Classifier
from workflow.extractor import Extractor
from workflow.normalizer import Normalizer
from workflow.validator import Validator
from workflow.indexer import Indexer
from workflow.reporter import Reporter
from workflow.orchestrator import Orchestrator

__all__ = [
    "Ingester",
    "Classifier",
    "Extractor",
    "Normalizer",
    "Validator",
    "Indexer",
    "Reporter",
    "Orchestrator",
]
seed_files.workflow/classifier.py:
"""Stage 2: Document classification — assigns type labels based on content."""

import re
from collections import Counter


# Category keyword definitions used for scoring
CATEGORY_KEYWORDS = {
    "financial": ["revenue", "profit", "loss", "quarterly", "earnings", "fiscal"],
    "legal": ["contract", "liability", "plaintiff", "defendant", "court", "statute"],
    "technical": ["algorithm", "implementation", "architecture", "deploy", "api", "system"],
    "medical": ["patient", "diagnosis", "treatment", "clinical", "symptom", "dosage"],
}


class Classifier:
    """Classifies documents into categories using keyword scoring."""

    def __init__(self, min_score=0.1):
        self.min_score = min_score
        self._cache = {}

    def classify(self, records):
        """Classify a list of ingestion records.

        Populates self._stopwords on first call, then scores each document
        against category keywords.
        """
        self._stopwords = {"the", "a", "an", "is", "are", "was", "were", "in",
                           "on", "at", "to", "for", "of", "and", "or", "but"}
        results = []
        for record in records:
            doc_id = record["id"]
            if doc_id in self._cache:
                results.append(self._cache[doc_id])
                continue
            scores = {}
            for category, keywords in CATEGORY_KEYWORDS.items():
                scores[category] = self._compute_score(record["body"], keywords)
            best_cat = max(scores, key=scores.get)
            best_score = scores[best_cat]
            classified = dict(record)
            if best_score >= self.min_score:
                classified["category"] = best_cat
                classified["confidence"] = round(best_score, 3)
            else:
                classified["category"] = "general"
                classified["confidence"] = 0.0
            classified["stage"] = "classified"
            self._cache[doc_id] = classified
            results.append(classified)
        return results

    def _compute_score(self, text, keywords):
        """Score text against a keyword list, ignoring stopwords."""
        words = self._normalize_text(text)
        total = len(words) if words else 1
        hits = sum(1 for w in words if w in keywords)
        return hits / total

    def _normalize_text(self, text):
        """Lowercase, strip punctuation, remove stopwords."""
        raw = re.sub(r"[^\w\s]", "", text.lower()).split()
        return [w for w in raw if w not in self._stopwords]
seed_files.workflow/validator.py:
"""Stage 5: Data validation — checks normalized data against rules."""

import re


# Required fields per category
REQUIRED_FIELDS = {
    "financial": ["amount", "date"],
    "legal": ["case_number", "date"],
    "technical": ["version"],
    "medical": ["dosage", "date"],
}

# Format patterns for validation
FORMAT_RULES = {
    "date": r"^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$",
    "amount": r"^[A-Z]{3}\s\d+\.\d{2}$",
    "version": r"^v\d+\.\d+\.\d+$",
    "dosage": r"^\d+\s(?:mg|ml|mcg)$",
    "case_number": r"^\d{2}-[A-Z]{2}-\d{4,6}$",
}


class Validator:
    """Validates normalized records against category-specific rules."""

    def __init__(self, strict=True):
        self.strict = strict
        self._errors = []

    def validate(self, records):
        """Validate each record's fields.

        Runs format validation first, then checks required fields.
        Records are marked valid/invalid based on accumulated errors.
        """
        results = []
        for record in records:
            validated = dict(record)
            category = record.get("category", "general")
            fields = record.get("fields", {})
            errors = []
            # Phase 1: validate formats of present fields
            fmt_errors = self._validate_format(fields)
            errors.extend(fmt_errors)
            # Phase 2: check required fields are present
            req_errors = self._validate_required(category, fields)
            errors.extend(req_errors)
            validated["validation_errors"] = errors
            validated["is_valid"] = len(errors) == 0
            validated["stage"] = "validated"
            self._errors.extend(errors)
            results.append(validated)
        return results

    def _validate_format(self, fields):
        """Check that present fields match their expected format."""
        errors = []
        for field_name, value in fields.items():
            if value is None or value == "":
                continue  # skip empty — not a format error
            pattern = FORMAT_RULES.get(field_name)
            if pattern and not re.match(pattern, str(value)):
                errors.append(f"format:{field_name}:invalid")
        return errors

    def _validate_required(self, category, fields):
        """Check that all required fields for the category are present."""
        errors = []
        required = REQUIRED_FIELDS.get(category, [])
        for field_name in required:
            value = fields.get(field_name)
            if value is None or value == "":
                errors.append(f"required:{field_name}:missing")
        return errors

    def get_error_summary(self):
        """Return a summary of all validation errors."""
        from collections import Counter
        counts = Counter(e.split(":")[0] for e in self._errors)
        return dict(counts)
seed_files.workflow/orchestrator.py:
"""Stage 8: Pipeline orchestrator — runs all stages in sequence."""

from workflow.ingester import Ingester
from workflow.classifier import Classifier
from workflow.extractor import Extractor
from workflow.normalizer import Normalizer
from workflow.validator import Validator
from workflow.indexer import Indexer
from workflow.reporter import Reporter


class Orchestrator:
    """Runs the full document processing pipeline."""

    def __init__(self, strict=True, min_score=0.1):
        self.ingester = Ingester(strict=strict)
        self.classifier = Classifier(min_score=min_score)
        self.extractor = Extractor()
        self.normalizer = Normalizer()
        self.validator = Validator(strict=strict)
        self.indexer = Indexer()
        self.reporter = Reporter()
        self._stage_counts = {}

    def run(self, raw_documents):
        """Execute the full pipeline and return a report.

        Stages: ingest -> classify -> extract -> normalize -> validate -> index -> report
        """
        ingested = self.ingester.ingest(raw_documents)
        self._stage_counts["ingested"] = len(ingested)

        classified = self.classifier.classify(ingested)
        self._stage_counts["classified"] = len(classified)

        extracted = self.extractor.extract(classified)
        self._stage_counts["extracted"] = len(extracted)

        normalized = self.normalizer.normalize(extracted)
        self._stage_counts["normalized"] = len(normalized)

        validated = self.validator.validate(normalized)
        self._stage_counts["validated"] = len(validated)

        valid_records = [r for r in validated if r.get("is_valid", False)]
        indexed = self.indexer.index(validated)
        self._stage_counts["indexed"] = len(indexed)

        error_summary = self.validator.get_error_summary()
        report = self.reporter.generate_report(validated, indexed, errors=None)
        report["stage_counts"] = self._stage_counts
        report["error_summary"] = error_summary
        return report

    def get_stage_counts(self):
        return dict(self._stage_counts)
seed_files.tests/test_workflow.py:
"""Tests for the document processing workflow pipeline."""

import pytest
from workflow.ingester import Ingester
from workflow.classifier import Classifier
from workflow.extractor import Extractor
from workflow.normalizer import Normalizer
from workflow.validator import Validator
from workflow.indexer import Indexer
from workflow.reporter import Reporter
from workflow.orchestrator import Orchestrator


FINANCIAL_DOC = {
    "title": "Q3 Earnings Report",
    "body": "The quarterly revenue was $1,250,000.00 for fiscal year. "
            "Earnings grew with profit margins. Filed on 3/15/2024. "
            "Acme Corp reported strong earnings from operations.",
    "source": "sec_filings",
}

LEGAL_DOC = {
    "title": "Contract Dispute Filing",
    "body": "Case 23-CV-44821 filed in court. The plaintiff Johnson alleged "
            "breach of contract. The defendant Smith denied all liability "
            "under the statute of limitations. Filed 6/10/2024.",
    "source": "court_records",
}

TECHNICAL_DOC = {
    "title": "API Migration Guide",
    "body": "System architecture upgraded to v2.5. The implementation uses "
            "a new algorithm for deploy. POST /api/v2/users endpoint added.",
    "source": "internal_docs",
}

MEDICAL_DOC = {
    "title": "Patient Treatment Summary",
    "body": "Patient received diagnosis of condition X. Treatment includes "
            "500mg daily dosage. Clinical symptom resolved on 9/1/2024.",
    "source": "health_records",
}


def _make_ingested(docs):
    """Helper: ingest documents to get base records."""
    return Ingester().ingest(docs)


def _make_classified(docs):
    """Helper: ingest + classify."""
    records = _make_ingested(docs)
    return Classifier().classify(records)


def _make_extracted(docs):
    """Helper: ingest + classify + extract."""
    classified = _make_classified(docs)
    return Extractor().extract(classified)


class TestClassifier:
    def test_compute_score_before_classify(self):
        """_compute_score should work even when called directly."""
        c = Classifier()
        # This should NOT raise AttributeError
        score = c._compute_score("the revenue was high with profit", ["revenue", "profit"])
        assert score > 0

    def test_normalize_text_standalone(self):
        """_normalize_text should work without calling classify first."""
        c = Classifier()
        result = c._normalize_text("The quick brown fox is a test")
        assert "the" not in result
        assert "quick" in result

    def test_classify_financial(self):
        """Financial document should be classified correctly."""
        records = _make_ingested([FINANCIAL_DOC])
        results = Classifier().classify(records)
        assert results[0]["category"] == "financial"
        assert results[0]["confidence"] > 0

    def test_classify_multiple_types(self):
        """Multiple document types should all classify correctly."""
        records = _make_ingested([FINANCIAL_DOC, LEGAL_DOC, TECHNICAL_DOC])
        results = Classifier().classify(records)
        categories = [r["category"] for r in results]
        assert "financial" in categories
        assert "legal" in categories
        assert "technical" in categories


class TestExtractor:
    def test_financial_entity_with_amount(self):
        """Entity should be kept when amount IS present in the text."""
        classified = _make_classified([FINANCIAL_DOC])
        results = Extractor().extract(classified)
        fields = results[0]["fields"]
        assert fields.get("amount") is not None

    def test_legal_party_with_case_number(self):
        """Party should be kept when case_number IS present."""
        classified = _make_classified([LEGAL_DOC])
        results = Extractor().extract(classified)
        fields = results[0]["fields"]
        assert fields.get("case_number") is not None
        # party depends on case_number — should NOT be invalidated
        assert fields.get("party") is not None, \
            "party was invalidated even though case_number exists"

    def test_field_count_accuracy(self):
        """field_count should reflect actually valid extracted fields."""
        classified = _make_classified([LEGAL_DOC])
        results = Extractor().extract(classified)
        fields = results[0]["fields"]
        actual_count = sum(1 for v in fields.values() if v is not None)
        assert results[0]["field_count"] == actual_count

    def test_extraction_order_independence(self):
        """Extraction result should not depend on field iteration order."""
        classified = _make_classified([LEGAL_DOC])
        extractor = Extractor()
        r1 = extractor.extract(classified)
        classified2 = _make_classified([LEGAL_DOC])
        extractor2 = Extractor()
        r2 = extractor2.extract(classified2)
        assert r1[0]["fields"] == r2[0]["fields"]


class TestNormalizer:
    def test_normalize_record_count(self):
        """Normalizing N records should produce exactly N results."""
        extracted = _make_extracted([FINANCIAL_DOC])
        results = Normalizer().normalize(extracted)
        assert len(results) == 1, \
            f"Expected 1 normalized record, got {len(results)}"

    def test_normalize_multiple_records(self):
        """Normalizing 4 records should produce exactly 4 results."""
        extracted = _make_extracted([FINANCIAL_DOC, LEGAL_DOC, TECHNICAL_DOC, MEDICAL_DOC])
        results = Normalizer().normalize(extracted)
        assert len(results) == 4, \
            f"Expected 4 normalized records, got {len(results)}"

    def test_normalized_count_matches(self):
        """Internal counter should match number of records processed."""
        extracted = _make_extracted([FINANCIAL_DOC, LEGAL_DOC])
        n = Normalizer()
        results = n.normalize(extracted)
        assert n._normalized_count == len(extracted), \
            f"Counter is {n._normalized_count}, expected {len(extracted)}"

    def test_currency_normalization(self):
        """$1,250,000.00 should normalize to 'USD 1250000.00'."""
        n = Normalizer()
        result = n.normalize_currency("$1,250,000.00")
        assert result == "USD 1250000.00", f"Got: {result}"

    def test_date_normalization(self):
        """Date should normalize from M/D/YYYY to YYYY-MM-DD."""
        n = Normalizer()
        result = n.normalize_date("3/15/2024")
        assert result == "2024-03-15"

    def test_normalize_pipeline_financial(self):
        """Full normalization of financial doc should produce valid amounts."""
        extracted = _make_extracted([FINANCIAL_DOC])
        results = Normalizer().normalize(extracted)
        assert len(results) == 1
        fields = results[0]["fields"]
        if fields.get("amount") is not None:
            assert fields["amount"].startswith("USD "), f"Got: {fields['amount']}"


class TestValidator:
    def test_missing_required_detected(self):
        """Missing required field should produce 'required' error."""
        v = Validator()
        fields = {"amount": "USD 100.00", "date": None}
        record = {"category": "financial", "fields": fields, "stage": "normalized"}
        results = v.validate([record])
        errors = results[0]["validation_errors"]
        error_types = [e.split(":")[0] for e in errors]
        assert "required" in error_types

    def test_format_not_checked_when_required_missing(self):
        """When required fields are missing, format errors should not appear."""
        v = Validator(strict=True)
        # amount has bad format AND date is missing (required)
        fields = {"amount": "bad_format", "date": None}
        record = {"category": "financial", "fields": fields, "stage": "normalized"}
        results = v.validate([record])
        errors = results[0]["validation_errors"]
        error_types = [e.split(":")[0] for e in errors]
        assert "required" in error_types
        assert "format" not in error_types, \
            f"Format errors should not appear when required fields missing: {errors}"

    def test_valid_document_passes(self):
        """A document with all required fields in correct format should pass."""
        v = Validator()
        fields = {"version": "v2.5.0"}
        record = {"category": "technical", "fields": fields, "stage": "normalized"}
        results = v.validate([record])
        assert results[0]["is_valid"] is True

    def test_error_summary(self):
        """Error summary should categorize errors correctly."""
        v = Validator()
        fields = {"amount": "bad", "date": None}
        record = {"category": "financial", "fields": fields, "stage": "normalized"}
        v.validate([record])
        summary = v.get_error_summary()
        assert isinstance(summary, dict)


class TestIndexer:
    def test_separate_instances_independent(self):
        """Two Indexer instances should have independent entry lists."""
        valid = [
            {"id": "a", "title": "Test A", "body": "hello world",
             "is_valid": True, "category": "general", "fields": {}},
        ]
        idx1 = Indexer()
        idx1.index(valid)
        count1 = idx1.get_entry_count()

        idx2 = Indexer()
        assert idx2.get_entry_count() == 0, \
            f"New indexer has {idx2.get_entry_count()} entries (expected 0)"

        idx2.index(valid)
        assert idx1.get_entry_count() == count1
        assert idx2.get_entry_count() == count1
        assert idx1.entries is not idx2.entries

    def test_index_only_valid_records(self):
        """Only records with is_valid=True should be indexed."""
        idx = Indexer()
        records = [
            {"id": "a", "title": "Test", "body": "hello", "is_valid": True,
             "category": "general", "fields": {}},
            {"id": "b", "title": "Bad", "body": "world", "is_valid": False,
             "category": "general", "fields": {}},
        ]
        results = idx.index(records)
        assert len(results) == 1
        assert results[0]["doc_id"] == "a"

    def test_search_finds_indexed(self):
        """Search should return indexed documents by term match."""
        idx = Indexer()
        records = [
            {"id": "x", "title": "Revenue Report", "body": "quarterly earnings",
             "is_valid": True, "category": "financial", "fields": {}},
        ]
        idx.index(records)
        results = idx.search("quarterly")
        assert len(results) > 0
        assert results[0]["doc_id"] == "x"

    def test_fresh_indexer_empty(self):
        """A freshly created Indexer should have zero entries."""
        idx = Indexer()
        assert idx.get_entry_count() == 0


class TestReporter:
    def test_section_order_canonical(self):
        """Report should have sections in canonical order."""
        records = [
            {"id": "a", "title": "T", "body": "B", "category": "technical",
             "fields": {"version": "v1.0.0"}, "is_valid": True,
             "validation_errors": [], "stage": "validated"},
        ]
        report = Reporter().generate_report(records, [])
        expected_order = ["overview", "categories", "validation",
                          "index_stats", "field_coverage", "timeline"]
        assert report["section_order"] == expected_order

    def test_report_has_all_sections(self):
        """Report should contain all required sections."""
        records = [
            {"id": "a", "title": "T", "body": "B", "category": "general",
             "fields": {}, "is_valid": True, "validation_errors": [],
             "stage": "validated"},
        ]
        report = Reporter().generate_report(records, [])
        assert "overview" in report["sections"]
        assert "categories" in report["sections"]
        assert "validation" in report["sections"]
        assert "field_coverage" in report["sections"]

    def test_overview_accuracy(self):
        """Overview section should have correct document counts."""
        records = [
            {"id": "a", "title": "A", "body": "x", "is_valid": True,
             "validation_errors": [], "fields": {}, "category": "financial"},
            {"id": "b", "title": "B", "body": "y", "is_valid": False,
             "validation_errors": ["required:date:missing"], "fields": {},
             "category": "legal"},
        ]
        report = Reporter().generate_report(records, [])
        overview = report["sections"]["overview"]
        assert overview["total_documents"] == 2
        assert overview["valid_documents"] == 1
        assert overview["invalid_documents"] == 1


class TestIntegration:
    def test_full_pipeline_single_doc(self):
        """Single document should flow through entire pipeline."""
        orch = Orchestrator()
        report = orch.run([TECHNICAL_DOC])
        assert report["stage_counts"]["ingested"] == 1
        assert report["stage_counts"]["classified"] == 1
        assert "sections" in report

    def test_full_pipeline_multiple_docs(self):
        """Multiple documents of different types should produce correct counts."""
        orch = Orchestrator()
        docs = [FINANCIAL_DOC, LEGAL_DOC, TECHNICAL_DOC, MEDICAL_DOC]
        report = orch.run(docs)
        assert report["stage_counts"]["ingested"] == 4
        overview = report["sections"]["overview"]
        assert overview["total_documents"] == 4

    def test_pipeline_indexer_isolation(self):
        """Running pipeline twice should not leak index entries."""
        orch1 = Orchestrator()
        orch1.run([FINANCIAL_DOC])
        count1 = orch1.indexer.get_entry_count()

        orch2 = Orchestrator()
        orch2.run([TECHNICAL_DOC])
        count2 = orch2.indexer.get_entry_count()

        # Each orchestrator's indexer should only have its own docs
        assert count2 <= 1, \
            f"Second orchestrator has {count2} entries (expected <= 1, got leak from first)"

    def test_pipeline_section_order(self):
        """Report from full pipeline should have canonical section ordering."""
        orch = Orchestrator()
        report = orch.run([FINANCIAL_DOC, LEGAL_DOC])
        expected = ["overview", "categories", "validation",
                    "index_stats", "field_coverage", "timeline"]
        assert report["section_order"] == expected

    def test_pipeline_record_count_preserved(self):
        """Each pipeline stage should preserve the record count."""
        orch = Orchestrator()
        report = orch.run([FINANCIAL_DOC, LEGAL_DOC, TECHNICAL_DOC])
        counts = report["stage_counts"]
        # Every stage should process the same number of records
        assert counts["ingested"] == 3
        assert counts["classified"] == 3
        assert counts["extracted"] == 3
        assert counts["normalized"] == 3
        assert counts["validated"] == 3

    def test_pipeline_no_format_errors_when_fields_missing(self):
        """Documents with missing required fields should not get format errors."""
        orch = Orchestrator()
        report = orch.run([FINANCIAL_DOC, LEGAL_DOC, TECHNICAL_DOC, MEDICAL_DOC])
        # This exercises the validator ordering bug via integration

ground_truth: Fix 6 structural bugs: (1) move _stopwords to __init__, (2) two-pass extraction, (3) dedent normalizer lines, (4) swap validator order, (5) class→instance var, (6) reorder report sections.

eval_spec.check_script_content:
#!/bin/bash
cd "$WORKDIR"
set +e
SCORE=0
TOTAL=12

# Check 1: All tests pass
OUTPUT=$(python3 -m pytest tests/test_workflow.py -v 2>&1)
PASSED=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -c " PASSED")
TOTAL_TESTS=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -cE " (PASSED|FAILED)")
if [ "$PASSED" -eq "$TOTAL_TESTS" ] && [ "$TOTAL_TESTS" -gt 0 ]; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: All tests pass ($PASSED/$TOTAL_TESTS)"
else
    echo "  FAIL: $PASSED/$TOTAL_TESTS tests pass"
    echo "$OUTPUT" | grep "FAILED" | head -5
fi

# Check 2: Classifier _stopwords in __init__ (not just in classify)
if python3 -c "
from workflow.classifier import Classifier
c = Classifier()
# _stopwords must exist before classify() is called
assert hasattr(c, '_stopwords'), 'No _stopwords in __init__'
assert len(c._stopwords) > 0, '_stopwords empty'
print('OK')
" 2>&1 | grep -q "OK"; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: Classifier _stopwords initialized in __init__"
else
    echo "  FAIL: Classifier _stopwords not in __init__"
fi

# Check 3: Extractor two-pass (party kept when case_number exists)
if python3 -c "
from workflow.ingester import Ingester
from workflow.classifier import Classifier
from workflow.extractor import Extractor
legal = {'title': 'Filing', 'body': 'Case 23-CV-44821 filed. The plaintiff Johnson alleged breach.', 'source': 'x'}
records = Ingester().ingest([legal])
classified = Classifier().classify(records)
extracted = Extractor().extract(classified)
fields = extracted[0]['fields']
assert fields.get('party') is not None, f'party invalidated: {fields}'
print('OK')
" 2>&1 | grep -q "OK"; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: Extractor preserves party when case_number exists"
else
    echo "  FAIL: Extractor invalidates party (single-pass bug)"
fi

# Check 4: Normalizer produces 1 record per input (not duplicates)
if python3 -c "
from workflow.ingester import Ingester
from workflow.classifier import Classifier
from workflow.extractor import Extractor
from workflow.normalizer import Normalizer
fin = {'title': 'Q3 Earnings', 'body': 'Revenue \$1,250,000.00 fiscal quarter. Filed 3/15/2024. Acme Corp earnings profit.', 'source': 'x'}
records = Ingester().ingest([fin])
classified = Classifier().classify(records)
extracted = Extractor().extract(classified)
normalized = Normalizer().normalize(extracted)
assert len(normalized) == 1, f'Expected 1, got {len(normalized)}'
print('OK')
" 2>&1 | grep -q "OK"; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: Normalizer produces 1 record per input"
else
    echo "  FAIL: Normalizer duplicates records (indentation bug)"
fi

# Check 5: Normalizer counter matches record count
if python3 -c "
from workflow.ingester import Ingester
from workflow.classifier import Classifier
from workflow.extractor import Extractor
from workflow.normalizer import Normalizer
docs = [
    {'title': 'T1', 'body': 'Revenue profit quarterly earnings fiscal. Filed 3/15/2024. Acme Corp.', 'source': 'x'},
    {'title': 'T2', 'body': 'Algorithm implementation architecture deploy api system.', 'source': 'y'},
]
records = Ingester().ingest(docs)
classified = Classifier().classify(records)
extracted = Extractor().extract(classified)
n = Normalizer()
results = n.normalize(extracted)
assert n._normalized_count == len(extracted), f'Counter {n._normalized_count} != {len(extracted)}'
print('OK')
" 2>&1 | grep -q "OK"; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: Normalizer counter accurate"
else
    echo "  FAIL: Normalizer counter wrong (indentation bug)"
fi

# Check 6: Validator checks required BEFORE format
if python3 -c "
from workflow.validator import Validator
v = Validator()
fields = {'amount': 'bad_format', 'date': None}
record = {'category': 'financial', 'fields': fields, 'stage': 'normalized'}
results = v.validate([record])
errors = results[0]['validation_errors']
types = [e.split(':')[0] for e in errors]
assert 'required' in types, f'No required error: {errors}'
assert 'format' not in types, f'Format error present when required missing: {errors}'
print('OK')
" 2>&1 | grep -q "OK"; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: Validator checks required before format"
else
    echo "  FAIL: Validator format check runs when required fields missing"
fi

# Check 7: Indexer instances are independent
if python3 -c "
from workflow.indexer import Indexer
idx1 = Indexer()
idx1.index([{'id': 'a', 'title': 'T', 'body': 'hello', 'is_valid': True, 'category': 'g', 'fields': {}}])
idx2 = Indexer()
assert idx2.get_entry_count() == 0, f'New indexer has {idx2.get_entry_count()} entries'
print('OK')
" 2>&1 | grep -q "OK"; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: Indexer instances independent"
else
    echo "  FAIL: Indexer leaks entries between instances (class var bug)"
fi

from workflow.reporter import Reporter
records = [{'id': 'a', 'title': 'T', 'body': 'B', 'category': 'technical',
            'fields': {'version': 'v1.0.0'}, 'is_valid': True,
            'validation_errors': [], 'stage': 'validated'}]
report = Reporter().generate_report(records, [])
expected = ['overview', 'categories', 'validation', 'index_stats', 'field_coverage', 'timeline']
assert report['section_order'] == expected, f'Got: {report[\"section_order\"]}'
print('OK')
" 2>&1 | grep -q "OK"; then

    echo "  PASS: Reporter section order canonical"
else
    echo "  FAIL: Reporter sections in wrong order"
fi

# Check 9: Source check - classifier __init__ has _stopwords
if grep -q "_stopwords" workflow/classifier.py && python3 -c "
import ast, sys
with open('workflow/classifier.py') as f:
    tree = ast.parse(f.read())
for node in ast.walk(tree):
    if isinstance(node, ast.FunctionDef) and node.name == '__init__':
        src = ast.dump(node)
        if '_stopwords' in src:
            print('OK')
            sys.exit(0)
sys.exit(1)
" 2>&1 | grep -q "OK"; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: Source: _stopwords assigned in __init__"
else
    echo "  FAIL: Source: _stopwords not in __init__"
fi

# Check 10: Source check - indexer entries is instance var (not class var)
if python3 -c "
import ast, sys
with open('workflow/indexer.py') as f:
    tree = ast.parse(f.read())
cls = [n for n in ast.walk(tree) if isinstance(n, ast.ClassDef) and n.name == 'Indexer'][0]
# Check no class-level 'entries = []'
for stmt in cls.body:
    if isinstance(stmt, ast.Assign):
        for target in stmt.targets:
            if isinstance(target, ast.Name) and target.id == 'entries':
                print('FAIL: class-level entries')
                sys.exit(1)
# Check __init__ has self.entries
for node in ast.walk(cls):
    if isinstance(node, ast.Assign):
        for target in node.targets:
            if isinstance(target, ast.Attribute) and isinstance(target.value, ast.Name) and target.value.id == 'self' and target.attr == 'entries':
                print('OK')
                sys.exit(0)
print('FAIL: no self.entries in __init__')
sys.exit(1)
" 2>&1 | grep -q "OK"; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: Source: entries is instance variable"
else
    echo "  FAIL: Source: entries still class variable"
fi

# Check 11: Full pipeline integration produces correct counts
if python3 -c "
from workflow.orchestrator import Orchestrator
docs = [
    {'title': 'Q3 Earnings', 'body': 'Revenue \$1,250,000.00 fiscal quarter earnings profit. 3/15/2024. Acme Corp.', 'source': 'x'},
    {'title': 'Filing', 'body': 'Case 23-CV-44821 filed in court. The plaintiff Johnson denied liability statute. 6/10/2024.', 'source': 'y'},
    {'title': 'API Guide', 'body': 'System architecture v2.5 implementation algorithm deploy. POST /api/v2/users endpoint.', 'source': 'z'},
]
orch = Orchestrator()
report = orch.run(docs)
counts = report['stage_counts']
assert counts['normalized'] == 3, f'normalized={counts[\"normalized\"]}'
assert counts['validated'] == 3, f'validated={counts[\"validated\"]}'
print('OK')
" 2>&1 | grep -q "OK"; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: Pipeline stage counts preserved"
else
    echo "  FAIL: Pipeline stage counts wrong"
fi

# Check 12: Pipeline isolation (two orchestrator runs don't leak)
if python3 -c "
from workflow.orchestrator import Orchestrator
o1 = Orchestrator()
o1.run([{'title': 'T1', 'body': 'Revenue profit quarterly earnings fiscal. 3/15/2024. Acme Corp.', 'source': 'x'}])
c1 = o1.indexer.get_entry_count()
o2 = Orchestrator()
o2.run([{'title': 'T2', 'body': 'Algorithm implementation architecture deploy api system.', 'source': 'y'}])
c2 = o2.indexer.get_entry_count()
assert c2 <= 1, f'Second orchestrator has {c2} entries, expected <=1'
print('OK')
" 2>&1 | grep -q "OK"; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: Pipeline isolation correct"
else
    echo "  FAIL: Pipeline leaks between orchestrator instances"
fi

echo ""
echo "Score: $SCORE/$TOTAL"

if [ "$SCORE" -ge 12 ]; then
    exit 0
else
    exit 1
fi
